bibliographic_data.title: PointAR: Efficient Lighting Estimation for Mobile Augmented Reality
bibliographic_data.abstract: We propose an efficient lighting estimation pipeline that is suitable to run on modern mobile devices, with comparable resource complexities to state-of-the-art mobile deep learning models. Our pipeline, PointAR, takes a single RGB-D image captured from the mobile camera and a 2D location in that image, and estimates 2nd order spherical harmonics coefficients. This estimated spherical harmonics coefficients can be directly utilized by rendering engines for supporting spatially variant indoor lighting, in the context of augmented reality. Our key insight is to formulate the lighting estimation as a point cloud-based learning problem directly from point clouds, which is in part inspired by the Monte Carlo integration leveraged by real-time spherical harmonics lighting. While existing approaches estimate lighting information with complex deep learning pipelines, our method focuses on reducing the computational complexity. Through both quantitative and qualitative experiments, we demonstrate that PointAR achieves lower lighting estimation errors compared to state-of-the-art methods. Further, our method requires an order of magnitude lower resource, comparable to that of mobile-specific DNNs.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a novel point cloud-based learning approach for lighting estimation on mobile devices, which achieves lower computational complexity and better performance compared to existing methods.
core_content.key_methodology: PointAR formulates the lighting estimation problem as a point cloud-based learning task. It uses an efficient mathematical model for view transformation and a compact deep learning model for estimating 2nd order spherical harmonics coefficients from limited scene information.
outcomes: Achieves lower lighting estimation errors compared to state-of-the-art methods
Requires significantly less computational resources, comparable to mobile-specific DNNs
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, the authors propose PointAR, an efficient lighting estimation pipeline for mobile augmented reality applications. The key challenge in mobile AR is accurately estimating lighting information from a partial view captured by a limited field-of-view camera. PointAR addresses this by breaking down the problem into two sub-problems: geometry-aware view transformation and point-cloud based learning. It uses spherical harmonics coefficients to represent diffuse irradiance maps, which can be directly utilized for rendering 3D objects under spatially variant lighting conditions. Through experiments, it is shown that PointAR outperforms existing methods in terms of both accuracy and computational efficiency.
